File inspection via file command: 'pcapng capture file - version 1.0'
tshark -r 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng -Y 'tcp.analysis.ack_rtt' -T fields -e frame.number -e tcp.analysis.ack_rtt -e tcp.stream | sort -k2 -gr | head -1
tshark RTT analysis of packet 27 in 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng
tshark TCP stream analysis: maximum RTT packet belongs to stream index 1
tshark -r 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng -Y 'tcp.stream eq 0' -T fields -e ip.src -e ip.dst -e tcp.srcport -e tcp.dstport
tshark -r 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng -Y 'tcp.stream eq 1' -T fields -e ip.src -e ip.dst -e tcp.srcport -e tcp.dstport

pi_items.verified_value: Official Wireshark Sample Captures repository (real network traffic)
pi_items.source: Downloaded from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/200722_tcp_anon.pcapng and referenced in install_wireshark.sh lines 95-98

privileged_info_summary: The 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng file is an authentic pcapng capture from the official Wireshark sample captures repository containing 35 packets with 2 TCP streams. Stream 0 (8 packets) connects 192.168.200.135:7875 to 192.168.200.21:2000, while Stream 1 (27 packets) connects 192.168.200.135:7876 to the same destination. The packet with maximum RTT latency is packet number 27 with an RTT value of 0.057657000 seconds (57.657 ms) in stream index 1. This is real network traffic data, not synthetic.
